title: Tom Brady brought jokes, nostalgia, and a comeback tease to Jimmy Fallon ahead of flag football debut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Tom Brady teased a return to competition on 'The Tonight Show' before his flag football debut in Los Angeles.
Brady discussed his transition from NFL player to TV broadcaster, focusing on the next generation of football talent.
The Fanatics Flag Football Classic, featuring Tom Brady and Rob Gronkowski, moved to Los Angeles due to Middle East conflicts.

title: This is the current state of the TSA at Logan Airport
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: More than 30 TSA employees have quit at Logan Airport as the government shutdown enters its second month.
Logan Airport has so far avoided long security lines despite staffing issues faced by other major airports.
Massport is introducing a new camera system to provide real-time TSA wait times, enhancing passenger convenience.

title: Man found dead in Brockton was in fight, then run over, DA says
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Lianes Brandao, 28, died in Brockton after a fight and a hit-and-run incident, according to authorities.
Brandao was found by police near Cardoso Cafe and later died at a Boston hospital, authorities reported.
The suspected vehicle identified was a gray 2013 Jeep Compass; the operator has been interviewed by police.

title: Pregnant, battling cancer — now she’s running the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Jennifer Capone Haberek, from New Hampshire, was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer while pregnant in 2024.
She is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for the Ellie Fund, which supported her during treatment.
Now cancer-free, Haberek aims to celebrate her two-year cancer-free milestone by completing the marathon.

title: A Korean trio of restaurants heads to Allston, starting with Sanbada
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Sanbada, part of a Korean restaurant trio, has opened in Allston, with Bombbada and Jinjee set to join.
Sanbada features a menu with fish sets, banchan, and a mix of Korean and Italian-style cooking techniques.
Bombbada will focus on late-night dining with trendy dishes, while Jinjee will offer a Korean BBQ experience.

title: The 5 most exciting books to read this spring — what’s on your list?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: "Sweetbitter Song" by Rosie Hewlett retells the Odyssey with a sapphic twist, releasing March 17, 2026.
"Under Water" by Tara Menon is a literary fiction exploring friendship and loss, set against natural disasters.
"Last Night in Brooklyn" by Xochitl Gonzalez captures Brooklyn's gentrification and a coming-of-age story, releasing April 21, 2026.

title: Vermont Green FC upset Portland Hearts of Pine in regional rivalry U.S. Open Cup clash
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Vermont Green FC defeated Portland Hearts of Pine 1-0 in the U.S. Open Cup, with David Ismail scoring.
Despite being in USL League Two, Vermont Green FC upset USL League One's Portland Hearts of Pine on home turf.
The match, played in 19-degree weather, marked the first meeting between the two recently founded clubs.

title: Police arrest 15-year-old in East Boston stabbing after he allegedly returns to the crime scene for his cellphone
author: John Waller
bullets: A 15-year-old suspect was arrested in East Boston after allegedly returning to a stabbing scene to retrieve his cellphone.
The victim was allegedly attacked by two ski-masked suspects and taken to a hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.
The teen faces charges of aggravated assault and armed robbery; officers found a ski mask and knives at the scene.

title: Woman, 45, killed in Newton crash
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A 45-year-old woman died in a two-car crash in Newton near Walnut Street and Judith Road, Tuesday afternoon.
Her vehicle collided with another car and then hit a tree; she was transported to a hospital and later died.
The second driver involved in the crash did not suffer serious injuries; the incident is under investigation.

title: 1 dead after car crashes into tree in Chelmsford
author: John Waller
bullets: A man died after his car crashed into a tree in Chelmsford around 12:30 p.m. on Tuesday.
The incident occurred near 75 Littleton Road, and the driver was pronounced dead at a local hospital.
The man's identity has not been released, and the crash is under ongoing investigation by authorities.
